Description: The fundamental building blocks of software engineering that every developer must understand deeply.

Skills.Title: Programming Languages
Skills.Effect: Programming languages are the foundation of all software. Mastery of multiple paradigms allows you to choose the best tool for the task.
BestPractices: Master Imperative, Functional, and OOP paradigms

Skills.Title: Data Structures & Algorithms
Skills.Effect: DSA is about efficiency and problem-solving. It separates a coder from a true software engineer.
BestPractices: Master Arrays, Linked Lists, Trees, and Graphs

Skills.Title: Web Development Basics
Skills.Effect: These are the languages of the web. Crucial for building cohesive, accessible, and high-performance products.

Skills.Title: Memory Management
Skills.Effect: Knowing how the Stack and Heap work helps you prevent memory leaks and write code that is respectful of machine resources.
BestPractices: Optimize for spatial and temporal locality

Skills.Title: Paradigms: OOP vs Functional
Skills.Effect: Different problems require different mental models. Understanding both allows for more flexible and maintainable architectures.
BestPractices: Use FP for data transformation and pure logic

Description: Understanding how data flows, how systems communicate, and how to scale infrastructure.

Skills.Title: Database Management
Skills.Effect: Data is at the heart of every application. Proper management ensures integrity and high performance.

Skills.Title: APIs & System Integration
Skills.Effect: Modern apps are rarely isolated. Mastering APIs allows your frontend to communicate with powerful backends and services.

Skills.Title: System Design & Architecture
Skills.Effect: Building scalable systems is hard. Understanding architectural patterns ensures your app can handle millions of users.

Skills.Title: Operating Systems & Networking
Skills.Effect: Knowing how memory management, file systems, and HTTP protocols work helps you debug low-level issues and optimize software.

Skills.Title: Observability & Logging
Skills.Effect: When things break in production, you need visibility. Monitoring allows you to diagnose issues in minutes instead of hours.

Description: The tools and workflows that ensure code quality, collaboration, and consistent delivery.

Skills.Title: Version Control (Git)
Skills.Effect: Git is the industry standard for collaboration and safety. It provides a history of every change made to a project.

Skills.Title: Automated Testing
Skills.Effect: Testing ensures that new changes don't break existing features, reducing technical debt and allowing faster deployment.
BestPractices: Implement Unit, Integration, and E2E tests
Achieve high test coverage for critical paths

Skills.Title: Mastering the Command Line
Skills.Effect: The CLI is a power-user's playground. It provides speed and automation capabilities far beyond what GUIs offer.
BestPractices: Automate repetitive tasks with shell scripts
Learn SSH for remote server management

Skills.Title: Concurrency & Parallelism
Skills.Effect: Modern hardware is multi-core. Mastering async logic allows you to write software that handles multiple operations simultaneously.

Skills.Title: Technical Debt & Refactoring
Skills.Effect: Refactoring is 'paying interest' on technical debt to keep the codebase healthy, modular, and maintainable over time.

Description: Modern challenges including cloud orchestration, security compliance, and AI-driven development.

Skills.Title: Cloud, Docker & DevOps
Skills.Effect: Ensures your application runs consistently across all environments, enabling seamless scaling and high availability.

Skills.Title: Security & Best Practices
Skills.Effect: Security is a core responsibility. Protecting user data is critical for trust and company reputation.

Skills.Title: Data Privacy & Compliance
Skills.Effect: In the modern era, data is a liability. Understanding GDPR/CCPA is critical for any developer handling user data.

Skills.Title: AI & LLM Force Multipliers
Skills.Effect: AI tools are multipliers. They allow you to automate boilerplate and focus on higher-level architectural design.

Skills.Title: Soft Skills & Teamwork
Skills.Effect: Coding is a team sport. Communication and documentation are as important as technical ability.
